children.heading: Assistance to internally displaced people in Ukraine
children.label: a Assistance to internally displaced people in Ukraine
A
children.text: the Committee on Foreign Relations, the Committee on Appropriations, and the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on Foreign Affairs, the Committee on Appropriations, and the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives.

children.label: A
children.text: helping to establish a functional and adequately resourced central registration system in Ukraine that can ensure coordination of efforts to provide assistance to internally displaced persons in different regions;

children.label: B
children.text: encouraging adoption of legislation in Ukraine that protects internally displaced persons from discrimination based on their status and provides simplified procedures for obtaining the new residency registration or other official documentation that is a prerequisite to receiving appropriate social payments under the laws of Ukraine, such as pensions and disability, child, and unemployment benefits; and

children.label: i
children.text: government agencies and independent groups that can provide assistance to such persons in various regions; and

children.label: ii
children.text: evacuation assistance available to persons seeking to flee armed conflict areas.

children.heading: Assistance through international organizations
children.label: 3 Assistance through international organizations
children.text: The President shall instruct the United States permanent representative or executive director, as the case may be, to the relevant United Nations voluntary agencies, including the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees and the United Nations Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs, and other appropriate international organizations, to use the voice and vote of the United States to support appropriate assistance for internally displaced persons in Ukraine.

children.heading: Assistance to the defense sector of Ukraine
children.label: b Assistance to the defense sector of Ukraine
children.text: The Secretary of State and the Secretary of Defense should assist entities in the defense sector of Ukraine to reorient exports away from customers in the Russian Federation and to find appropriate alternative markets for those entities in the defense sector of Ukraine that have already significantly reduced exports to and cooperation with entities in the defense sector of the Russian Federation.

children.heading: Assistance to address the energy crisis in Ukraine
children.label: c Assistance to address the energy crisis in Ukraine

children.heading: Plan required
children.label: A Plan required
children.text: The Secretary of State and the Secretary of Energy, in collaboration with the Administrator of the United States Agency for International Development and the Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency, shall work with officials of the Government of Ukraine to develop a short-term emergency energy assistance plan designed to help Ukraine address the potentially severe short-term heating fuel and electricity shortages facing Ukraine in 2014 and 2015.

children.label: i
children.text: the acquisition of short-term, emergency fuel supplies;

children.label: ii
children.text: the repair or replacement of infrastructure that could impede the transmission of electricity or transportation of fuel;

children.label: iii
children.text: the prioritization of the transportation of fuel supplies to the areas where such supplies are needed most;

children.label: iv
children.text: streamlining emergency communications throughout national, regional, and local governments to manage the potential energy crisis resulting from heating fuel and electricity shortages;

children.label: v
children.text: forming a crisis management team within the Government of Ukraine to specifically address the potential crisis, including ensuring coordination of the team’s efforts with the efforts of outside governmental and nongovernmental entities providing assistance to address the potential crisis; and

children.label: vi
children.text: developing a public outreach strategy to facilitate preparation by the population and communication with the population in the event of a crisis.

children.label: i
children.text: procurement and transport of emergency fuel supplies, including reverse pipeline flows from Europe;

children.label: ii
children.text: provision of technical assistance for crisis planning, crisis response, and public outreach;

children.label: iii
children.text: repair of infrastructure to enable the transport of fuel supplies;

children.label: iv
children.text: repair of power generating or power transmission equipment or facilities;

children.label: v
children.text: procurement and installation of compressors or other appropriate equipment to enhance short-term natural gas production;

children.label: vi
children.text: procurement of mobile electricity generation units;

children.label: vii
children.text: conversion of natural gas heating facilities to run on other fuels, including alternative energy sources; and

children.label: viii
children.text: provision of emergency weatherization and winterization materials and supplies.

children.heading: Reduction of Ukraine’s reliance on energy imports
children.label: 2 Reduction of Ukraine’s reliance on energy imports

children.heading: Plans required
children.label: A Plans required
children.text: The Secretary of State, in collaboration with the Secretary of Energy and the Administrator of the United States Agency for International Development, shall work with officials of the Government of Ukraine to develop medium- and long-term plans to increase energy production and efficiency to increase energy security by helping Ukraine reduce its dependence on natural gas imported from the Russian Federation.

children.label: i
children.text: improve corporate governance and unbundling of state-owned oil and gas sector firms;

children.label: ii
children.text: increase production from natural gas fields and from other sources, including renewable energy;

children.label: iii
children.text: license new oil and gas blocks transparently and competitively;

children.label: iv
children.text: modernize oil and gas upstream infrastructure; and

children.label: v
children.text: improve energy efficiency.

children.heading: Prioritization
children.label: C Prioritization
children.text: The Secretary of State, the Administrator of the United States Agency for International Development, and the Secretary of Energy should, during fiscal years 2015 through 2018, work with other donors, including multilateral agencies and nongovernmental organizations, to prioritize, to the extent practicable and as appropriate, the provision of assistance from such donors to help Ukraine to improve energy efficiency, increase energy supplies produced in Ukraine, and reduce reliance on energy imports from the Russian Federation, including natural gas.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: D Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated $50,000,000 in the aggregate for fiscal years 2016 through 2018 to carry out activities under this paragraph.

children.heading: Support from the United States International Development Finance Corporation
children.label: 3 Support from the United States International Development Finance Corporation
A
children.text: prioritize, to the extent practicable, support for investments to help increase energy efficiency, develop domestic oil and natural gas reserves, improve and repair electricity infrastructure, and develop renewable and other sources of energy in Ukraine; and

children.label: B
children.text: implement procedures for expedited review and, as appropriate, approval, of applications for loans, loan guarantees, and insurance for such investments.

children.heading: Support by the World Bank Group and the European Bank for Reconstruction and Development
children.label: 4 Support by the World Bank Group and the European Bank for Reconstruction and Development
A
children.text: to invest in, and increase their efforts to promote investment in, projects to improve energy efficiency, improve and repair electricity infrastructure, develop domestic oil and natural gas reserves, and develop renewable and other sources of energy in Ukraine; and

children.label: B
children.text: to stimulate private investment in such projects.

children.heading: Assistance to civil society in Ukraine
children.label: d Assistance to civil society in Ukraine
A
children.text: strengthen the organizational and operational capacity of democratic civil society in Ukraine;

children.label: B
children.text: support the efforts of independent media outlets to broadcast, distribute, and share information in all regions of Ukraine;

children.label: C
children.text: counter corruption and improve transparency and accountability of institutions that are part of the Government of Ukraine; and

children.label: D
children.text: provide support for democratic organizing and election monitoring in Ukraine.

children.label: A
children.text: the Committee on Foreign Relations and the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on Foreign Affairs and the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 3 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary of State $20,000,000 for fiscal year 2016 to carry out this subsection.

children.heading: Transparency requirements
children.label: 4 Transparency requirements
children.text: Any assistance provided pursuant to this subsection shall be conducted in as transparent of a manner as possible, consistent with the nature and goals of this subsection. The President shall provide a briefing on the activities funded by this subsection at the request of the committees specified in paragraph (2).
